rules.summary: Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to:
In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:

A firm must ensure its governing body is ultimately responsible for the firm’s compliance with the PRA rules, FSMA and all applicable laws, rules, regulations and administrative provisions deriving fr
1. (1) A firm must have in place an effective system of governance which provides for sound and prudent management of its business.
A firm’s system of governance must be proportionate to the nature, scale and complexity of its operations.
A firm must:

A firm must establish, implement and maintain documented policies and adequate procedures to ensure that all persons who effectively run the firm or have other key functions are at all times fit and p
The written policies on risk-management referred to in 2.4(1) must include:
A firm must take reasonable steps to ensure continuity and regularity in the performance of its activities, including the development of contingency plans. To that end, the firm must employ approp
A firm must engage a broad set of qualities and competences when recruiting members to the governing body.
A firm must put in place a policy promoting diversity on the governing body.
A firm that maintains a website must explain on the website how it complies with the requirements of 2.7 and [2.8](
1. (1) A firm must establish, implement, and maintain an effective risk-management system comprising strategies, processes and reporting procedures necessary to identify, measure, monitor, manage and
A firm must ensure that the areas referred to in 3.1(2)(c) include all of the following policies:
As regards asset-liability management, a firm must:
A firm must submit the assessments referred to in 3.2 as part of the information reported annually in accordance wi
As regards investment risk, a firm must demonstrate that it complies with the Investments Part of the PRA Rulebook.
1. (1) A firm must provide for a risk-management __function that is structured in such a way as to facilitate the implementation of the risk-management system.

In addition to the requirements referred to in 3.6, for the purposes of the calculation of technical provisions and the SCR

For the purpose of assessing the appropriateness of external credit rating assessments used in the calculation of technical provisions and the SCR by way of additional assessments referred to in [3.6]

A firm must ensure that its risk-management function covers the additional assessments in accordance with the risk management policy referred to in [3.6B](/pra-rules/conditions-governing-business#8dff
When carrying out the additional assessments referred to in 3.6B, a firm must use information that is derived from reliable
1. (1) In accordance with 2.4, a firm must at least annually review the additional assessments referred to in [3.6B](/pra-r
A firm must document the following:
A firm that has received internal model permission must ensure that its risk-management function covers the following additional tasks:
1. (1) A firm must conduct an ORSA as part of its risk-management system.
1. (1) A firm must ensure that the ORSA referred to in 3.8(1) is forward-looking and includes all of the following elements
A firm must make the ORSA an integral part of its business strategy and take the ORSA into account on an ongoing basis in its strategic decisions.
A firm must perform the ORSA regularly and without delay following any significant change in its risk profile.
A firm must inform the PRA of the results of each ORSA in the form of an ORSA report in accordance with Reporting 2.5A(2)(a).
The ORSA report referred to at 3.11 must include all of the following:
1. (1) A firm must have in place an effective internal control system.
1. (1) A firm must ensure that the compliance function required by 4.1(2)(d) establishes a compliance policy and a complian
The duties of the compliance function referred to in 4.1A(1) must include:
A firm must have internal processes and procedures in place to ensure the appropriateness, completeness and accuracy of the data used in the calculation of its technical provisions.
A firm must have processes and procedures in place to ensure that the best estimate and the assumptions underlying the calculation of the best estimate are regularly compared against experience.
1. (1) A firm must provide for an effective internal audit function.
A firm must ensure that the internal audit function referred to in 5.1 includes all of the following tasks:
A firm must ensure that any person carrying out the internal audit function does not assume responsibility for any other function.
Notwithstanding 5.3, and in particular by respecting the principle of proportionality, a firm may allow the persons carryin
1. (1) A firm must provide for an effective actuarial function to:
A firm must provide for an actuarial function that, in coordinating the calculation of the technical provisions, includes all of the following tasks:

A firm must ensure that the actuarial function assesses whether the methodologies and assumptions used in the calculation of the technical provisions are appropriate for the specific lines of business

A firm must ensure that the actuarial function assesses whether the information technology systems used in the calculation of technical provisions sufficiently support the actuarial and statistical pr

1. (1) A firm must ensure that the actuarial function, when comparing best estimates against experience, reviews the quality of past best estimates and uses the insights gained from this assessment to
A firm must ensure that:

A firm must ensure that the opinion on the overall underwriting policy to be expressed by the actuarial function referred to in [6.1(1)(g)](/pra-rules/conditions-governing-business#01665ebb4e044fd8a1b

A firm must ensure that the opinion on the adequacy of reinsurance arrangements to be expressed by the actuarial function in accordance with [6.1(1)(h)](/pra-rules/conditions-governing-business#01665e
1. (1) A firm must ensure that the actuarial function produces a written report to be submitted to the governing body, at least annually.

If a firm outsources a function or any insurance or reinsurance activity, it remains fully responsible for discharging all of its obligations under the PRA rules, FSMA and any other laws, rules, regul

A firm must not outsource a critical or important operational function or activity in such a way as to lead to any of the following:

The written agreement referred to in 7.5(3) must clearly state all of the following requirements:
A firm that is outsourcing critical or important operational functions or activities must fulfil all of the following requirements:
A firm must not enter into a contract of finite reinsurance (either as a cedant or a reinsurer) or pursue finite reinsurance activities unless it is able to properly identify, measure, monitor
1. (1) A firm , other than a pure reinsurer , must not carry on any commercial business other than insurance business and activities directly arising from that business.
A pure reinsurer must not carry on any business other than the business of reinsurance and related operations.
A firm must not enter into a contract of long-term insurance unless it is satisfied, on reasonable actuarial assumptions, that the premiums receivable shall be sufficient:
